discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tape driven by a sharp semiconductor sell-off (SMH -3.78%) after Samsung earnings disappointed, with QQQ down nearly 2% while defensives (XLV, XLE, XLU, XLP) caught bids. Defense names are also under heavy pressure despite generally supportive geopolitical backdrop, suggesting profit-taking after strong recent runs. Biotech is a bright spot (XBI +1.9%).

notable_index_moves: SMH -3.78% on Samsung AI earnings miss — dragging semi-equipment and small-cap chip names
XLE +2.84% on U.S. revoking Iran oil sales authorization after tanker attacks
ITA -2.26% — defense group weak despite Greenland/NATO headlines, looks like profit-taking after a strong run
XBI +1.9% — biotech rotation into risk-off tape

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-equipment sympathy sell-off on Samsung AI earnings miss
discoveries.thesis: ONTO is a metrology/inspection play with meaningful exposure to memory and advanced packaging capex, both directly tied to Samsung's spending posture. A 5%+ decline in SMH plus a Samsung AI-specific disappointment justifies the drawdown; the extended 5-day slide (down ~20%) suggests the derating is broader than one day of tape.
discoveries.what_confirms: Follow-through selling if peers (KLAC, LRCX, CAMT) confirm memory-capex cut narrative; sell-side estimate cuts on Samsung capex flowing through
discoveries.what_kills: TSMC/SK Hynix reaffirming capex or ONTO issuing a positive pre-announcement; rebound in HBM demand commentary
discoveries.what_to_learn: Samsung-specific AI disappointment tends to hit memory-exposed metrology (ONTO, CAMT) harder than logic-exposed equipment (KLAC, AMAT) — worth separating within the basket
discoveries.catalyst: Samsung earnings AI miss + broad semi-equipment sell-off
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: News confirms sector-wide chip selloff on Samsung; no company-specific 8-K, move consistent with SMH beta.
research_pointers: Check ONTO's most recent disclosed memory/advanced-packaging revenue mix
Compare 5-day drawdown vs. KLAC/CAMT/AMAT — is ONTO an outlier or in line?
Look for buy-side upgrades on entry points after the 20% pullback

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Sharp single-day decline in defense name after multi-week rally, no company-specific catalyst
discoveries.thesis: AVAV down hard despite still being up sharply over 5 days and against a geopolitically supportive backdrop (Iran oil sanctions restored, Greenland/NATO headlines). No 8-K, no earnings, no company news — this reads as profit-taking on an extended run rather than fundamental deterioration. Fade candidate if defense tape stabilizes.
discoveries.what_confirms: Peer defense names (KTOS, MRCY, DCO also down mid-single-digits today) confirming this is basket-wide profit-taking with no fundamental thread; short interest data showing crowded long positioning being unwound
discoveries.what_kills: Discovery of a specific negative catalyst (contract loss, DoD budget headline hitting drone programs); if the sell-off continues on rising volume tomorrow, it's likely institutional distribution not just noise
discoveries.what_to_learn: When four defense names sell off simultaneously with no headline and the tape has been strong, it's almost always positioning-driven and often reverses within a week
discoveries.catalyst: No specific catalyst found; group-wide defense weakness with no news thread
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty; no 8-K, no earnings; multiple defense peers (KTOS, MRCY, DCO) moving in lockstep suggests group unwind.
research_pointers: Check if any DoD budget or CR news hit drone/UAS line items
Compare AVAV's 5-day gain vs. peers — was it the most extended before today?
Options positioning: any large put buying today or crowded call unwind?

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap-exposed name selling off on Samsung/chip weakness
discoveries.thesis: AEIS derives significant revenue from semiconductor capital equipment (power delivery for etch/deposition tools). A ~17% five-day drawdown alongside broader semi-cap weakness is consistent with sector derating. Magnitude looks appropriate given the exposure and prior valuation.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side capex cuts flowing through AEIS models; further weakness in AMAT/LRCX
discoveries.what_kills: Reaffirmation of 2026 semi WFE from major foundry customers; AEIS data-center/industrial segments offsetting semi weakness
discoveries.catalyst: Samsung earnings miss triggering semi-cap sector derating
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move tracks SMH beta and sector derating narrative from news feed.
research_pointers: Segment breakout: what % of AEIS revenue is semi vs. data center vs. industrial?
Compare drawdown vs. peers (MKSI, ICHR) to see if AEIS is outlier

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap biotech rallying with sector on risk-off day, no visible catalyst
discoveries.thesis: BEAM up 7%+ on above-average volume with no 8-K and no earnings scheduled. XBI is up 1.9% so some sector tailwind exists, but BEAM is outperforming the sector by 5%+ which suggests something more specific — either a data leak, an unnoticed press release, or partnership speculation. Without concrete evidence I can't articulate either bull or bear at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: A base-editing data readout, partnership announcement, or M&A rumor surfacing in next 24-48h
discoveries.what_kills: Simple sector rotation with no company-specific news over the next few sessions
discoveries.catalyst: No visible catalyst; outperforming XBI meaningfully
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; XBI +1.9% explains ~2pts of the move but not all of it.
research_pointers: Check biotech Twitter/StockTwits for any base-editing rumors
Scan for ASH/ASGCT abstracts or scheduled data readouts
Look for unusual call option activity

discoveries.name: Builders FirstSource
discoveries.setup: Housing-adjacent name extending multi-day decline with no catalyst
discoveries.thesis: BLDR down ~13% over 5 days with no 8-K or news. Could reflect rate/housing sentiment shift, tariff-linked lumber cost concerns (Trump tariff post on autos suggests continued tariff regime), or channel checks. Without a concrete thread I can't commit to a direction — the persistence of the drawdown is notable but not diagnostic.
discoveries.what_confirms: Housing starts data weakness, or a peer (LPX, WY) confirming demand softness
discoveries.what_kills: Rate cut expectations firming and homebuilder ETF (XHB) stabilizing
discoveries.catalyst: No specific catalyst; multi-day derating in housing-adjacent name
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; multi-day slide suggests fundamental repricing but no evidence to attribute it.
research_pointers: Check XHB and homebuilder tape for correlation
Look for any analyst downgrades in the past week
Mortgage rate moves and pending home sales data

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Samsung AI earnings miss triggering semi-cap sector selloff
U.S. revokes Iran oil sales authorization after tanker attacks

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump claims Toyota moving production from Mexico to Texas, credits tariffs
trump_signals.note: Reinforces ongoing tariff regime narrative. Marginal read-through: continued cost pressure on auto/manufacturing supply chains sourcing from Mexico; longer-term positive for U.S. industrial real estate and Texas-based suppliers. Not a discrete tradeable event on its own.

ai_announcements.headline: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic's Claude Cowork agent now supports async workflows across mobile/web, extending coding-agent paradigm into general office/knowledge work tasks.
ai_announcements.notes: No stocks in today's mover list have clean direct exposure to Claude Cowork's expansion (e.g., MSFT, GOOGL, CRM, NOW aren't in the list). Flagging as Anthropic-related for bias tracking but no actionable signal from provided market data.

ai_announcements.headline: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
ai_announcements.summary: MSFT reducing AI spend and shifting to in-house models, part of broader hyperscaler capex discipline theme.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Onto sells metrology to HBM/advanced packaging - AI capex slowdown narrative hurts sentiment but SK Hynix IPO and HBM demand remain strong; sell-off likely sentiment-driven on hyperscaler capex fears
research_pointers: Check if HBM/advanced packaging orders are affected by MSFT's model efficiency push
Compare to SK Hynix IPO signal - strong memory demand contradicts capex-cut thesis
5-day -20% suggests broader semi-cap unwind, not single-name news

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: AEIS power conversion sells into semi fab equipment and data center; AI cost-cutting narrative hits both. But actual fab equipment orders driven by TSMC/Samsung/Intel capex, not MSFT model choice
research_pointers: 5-day -17% signals persistent AI-capex-peak narrative
Verify data center power exposure vs semi-equipment exposure mix

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Lattice FPGAs are used in edge/comms/server, not directly displaced by MSFT internal model shift; sell-off likely sympathy with broader AI-semi rotation
research_pointers: Confirm FPGA exposure to hyperscaler capex vs comms/industrial

ai_announcements.headline: First American autonomous ground vehicles fighting in Ukraine (Forterra)
ai_announcements.summary: Autonomous ground combat vehicles deployed at scale by a private company, potentially signaling competition for defense primes/small drone makers.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: AVAV dominates small UAS (Switchblade, Puma) - Forterra ground vehicles are complementary, not direct substitute. AVAV +17% over 5 days suggests profit-taking after a run, not fundamental Forterra threat
research_pointers: Check for AVAV-specific news (earnings, contract loss)
Forterra is UGV not UAS - product overlap is limited
5-day +17% before -8% = likely mean reversion

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Kratos does tactical drones/targets/hypersonics - Forterra UGVs don't overlap. Sell-off likely defense-sector rotation or profit-taking (KTOS +7% 5d)
research_pointers: Look for sector-wide defense weakness today
Check if any contract/budget headline drove group move

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Mercury is embedded processing for defense - AI/autonomy proliferation is TAILWIND long-term (more compute per platform)
research_pointers: Group move with AVAV/KTOS/DCO suggests defense sector rotation, not Forterra-specific

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Ducommun makes aerostructures/electronics - unrelated to autonomous ground vehicles
research_pointers: Likely defense sector sympathy move, not AI-driven

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Defense cluster (AVAV/KTOS/MRCY/DCO) all down 5-8% together strongly suggests a sector-specific catalyst (budget headline, group rotation) rather than the Forterra article. Recommend Michael check for a defense-budget or geopolitical headline not in this news feed before trading these as AI-overreactions. BEAM/PTCT moves are biotech, unrelated to AI news. JBLU/BLDR/AIT/AYI moves not linked to any AI announcement in feed.
